navigation # Examples [Permalink: Examples](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#examples) A collection of examples of Tauri apps using frontends written in Rust or JavaScript. These examples are a great way to see how Tauri works with a variety of frameworks. Uses the template project of each framework. ## How to run [Permalink: How to run](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#how-to-run) Ensure that you have read the README of the example to check that your first-time setup is done. The examples assume that this git repository was cloned in the root of the [tauri repo](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri) clone on your machine. The recommended way of running the examples is using [mask](https://github.com/jakedeichert/mask): ``` $ cargo install mask $ git clone https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri $ cd tauri $ mask prepare $ mask list smoke-tests # this will show all the examples names $ mask run smoke-test some-example-name # run an example listed on the previous command ``` ## Error reporting [Permalink: Error reporting](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#error-reporting) Please report all library errors at [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri) ## License [Permalink: License](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#license) Everything in this repo is MIT License unless otherwise specified. ## About A collection of frameworks used as a suite of smoke-tests for tauri ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/tauri-apps/smoke-tests#MIT-1-ov-file) ### Uh oh! web.title: Tests - Tauri
web.description: Tauri offers support for both unit and integration testing utilizing a mock runtime. Under the mock runtime, native webview libraries are not executed.
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://v2.tauri.app/develop/tests/#_top)

# Tests

Tauri offers support for both unit and integration testing utilizing a mock runtime. Under the mock runtime, native
webview libraries are not executed. [See more about the mock runtime here](https://v2.tauri.app/develop/tests/mocking/).

Tauri also provides support for end-to-end testing support utilizing the WebDriver protocol. Both desktop and mobile
work with it, except for macOS which does not provide a desktop WebDriver client. [See more about WebDriver support here](https://v2.tauri.app/develop/tests/webdriver/).

We offer [tauri-action](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-action) to help run GitHub actions, but any sort of CI/CD runner can be used with Tauri as long as each
platform has the required libraries installed to compile against.

* * *

web.title: Tauri (6) — Invoke desktop application functionality through the ...
web.description: In a Tauri V2 application, it's possible to open the app via a specific URL in the browser by configuring the protocol and relevant settings.
web.markdown: ## Introduction Most of the online articles focus on Tauri V1. This article primarily discusses the implementation in Tauri V2. In a Tauri V2 application, it's possible to open the app via a specific URL in the browser by configuring the protocol and relevant settings. Let’s walk through the steps to achieve this functionality. ## Install Plugins ### Install `@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link` This plugin is used to set the Tauri application as the default handler for a specific URL. **Method 1: Modify `Cargo.toml`** Add the following to the `src-tauri/Cargo.toml` file: ``` [dependencies] tauri-plugin-deep-link = "2.0.0" # alternatively with Git: tauri-plugin-deep-link = { git = "https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace", branch = "v2" } ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode **Method 2: Install via JS** ``` pnpm add @tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link # or npm add @tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link # or yarn add @tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link # alternatively with Git: pnpm add https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-plugin-deep-link#v2 # or npm add https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-plugin-deep-link#v2 # or yarn add https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-plugin-deep-link#v2 ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ### Install `tauri-plugin-single-instance` This ensures that only a single instance of the Tauri application is running. **Configuration: Modify `Cargo.toml`** Add the following to the `src-tauri/Cargo.toml` file: ``` [dependencies] tauri-plugin-single-instance = "2.0.0" # alternatively with Git: tauri-plugin-single-instance = { git = "https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace", branch = "v2" } ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## Configure Custom Protocol Tauri supports using custom protocols like `tauri://` to open content in the application via the browser. Add the following to the `src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` file: ``` "plugins": { "features": { "protocol": ["all"] }, "window": {}, "websocket": {}, "shell": {}, "globalShortcut": {}, "deep-link": { "schema": "coco", "mobile": [\ { "host": "app.infini.cloud", "pathPrefix": ["/open"] },\ { "host": "localhost:9000" }\ ], "desktop": { "schemes": ["coco"] } } } ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## Implement Custom Protocol Logic Add the following to the `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` file: ``` use tauri_plugin_deep_link::DeepLinkExt; #[derive(Clone, serde::Serialize)] struct Payload { args: Vec, cwd: String, } #[cfg_attr(mobile, tauri::mobile_entry_point)] pub fn run() { let mut ctx = tauri::generate_context!(); tauri::Builder::default() .plugin(tauri_plugin_shell::init()) .plugin(tauri_plugin_deep_link::init()) .plugin(tauri_plugin_single_instance::init(|app, argv, cwd| { app.emit("single-instance", Payload { args: argv, cwd }) .unwrap(); })) .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![\ ]) .setup(|app| { init(app.app_handle()); #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] app.set_activation_policy(ActivationPolicy::Accessory); #[cfg(any(target_os = "linux", all(debug_assertions, windows)))] app.deep_link().register_all()?; app.deep_link().on_open_url(|event| { dbg!(event.urls()); }); Ok(()) }) .run(ctx) .expect("error while running tauri application"); } ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## Permission Configuration Add the following to the `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` file: ``` "permissions": [\ "oauth:allow-start",\ "deep-link:allow-get-current",\ "deep-link:default",\ "deep-link:allow-register"\ ] ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## MacOS Configuration In a Tauri project, `src-tauri/Info.plist` is a macOS-specific file that defines key properties and behaviors for the application, such as the name, permissions, URL schemes, etc. Modify the `src-tauri/Info.plist` file: ``` NSCameraUsageDescription Request camera access for WebRTC NSMicrophoneUsageDescription Request microphone access for WebRTC CFBundleIdentifier rs.coco.app NSPrefPaneIconLabel coco-ai CFBundleURLTypes CFBundleURLName rs.coco.app CFBundleURLSchemes coco ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## Frontend Implementation ### Tauri APP Code: ``` import { onOpenUrl, getCurrent as getCurrentDeepLinkUrls, } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link"; const handleUrl = (url: string) => { try { const urlObject = new URL(url); console.error("urlObject:", urlObject); switch (urlObject.pathname) { case "oauth_callback": const code = urlObject.searchParams.get("code"); const provider = urlObject.searchParams.get("provider"); // Implement logic here break; default: console.log("Unhandled deep link path:", urlObject.pathname); } } catch (err) { console.error("Failed to parse URL:", err); } }; // Fetch the initial deep link intent useEffect(() => { getCurrentDeepLinkUrls() .then((urls) => { if (urls && urls.length > 0) { handleUrl(urls[0]); } }) .catch((err) => console.error("Failed to get initial URLs:", err)); const unlisten = onOpenUrl((urls) => handleUrl(urls[0])); return () => { unlisten.then((fn) => fn()); }; }, []); ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ### Browser Web Code: ``` In order to continue, please click here to launch Coco AI ``` Enter fullscreen modeExit fullscreen mode ## Use Cases 1. **Triggering desktop app logic via web pages.** 2. **Integrating OAuth login, payment callbacks, etc., that require browser interaction.** 3. **Cross-platform data exchange.** 4. **Online documentation and help center.** 5. **Dynamic loading and updates.** ## Summary With the steps above, we’ve implemented the functionality to open a Tauri app from the browser using a custom protocol, supporting flexible configuration and multi-platform compatibility. ## References 1. [@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link) 2. [tauri-plugin-single-instance](https://crates.io/crates/tauri-plugin-single-instance) 3. [Coco App](https://github.com/infinilabs/coco-app) ## Open Source Recently, I’ve been working on a project based on Tauri called [Coco](https://github.com/infinilabs/coco-app). It’s **open source** and under continuous improvement. I’d love your support—please give the project a free **star** 🌟! This is my first Tauri project, and I’ve been learning while exploring. I look forward to connecting with like-minded individuals to share experiences and grow together! - Official website: [coco.rs/](https://coco.rs/) - Frontend repo: [github.com/infinilabs/coco-app](https://github.com/infinilabs/coco-app) - Backend repo: [github.com/infinilabs/coco-server](https://github.com/infinilabs/coco-server) Thank you for your support and attention! # [Would an app developed using Tauri, be difficult to functional test using playwright?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/77823389/would-an-app-developed-using-tauri-be-difficult-to-functional-test-using-playwr)

The developers of one of the apps I am functional testing are moving away from Electron (NODEJS) + Angular (JS) to WebView2 (.NET) + Angular (JS) by using Tauri 1.5. We have a functional test suite written using Playwright for the app developed using Electron (NODEJS) + Angular (JS). It was raised that Playwright may not be used to test the functionality once the app is built using Tauri.

I believe irrespective of how the app is being built, the desktop app can be functionally tested using the same playwright framework we already have. I looked around and Googled, but I could not find any information about that.

Can there be such limitations when it comes to functional testing? Isn't that the limitations come in when the code is unit tested while building?

**UPDATE:**

A bit of search taught me that Playwright would only work with the desktop apps that are built with Electron. Electron uses Chromium as its rendering engine, which is similar to how Playwright interacts with browsers.
Since my app is built using Tauri, and its underlying technologies differ from Electrons, Playwright may not support Tauri yet. Has anyone tried this combination yet?

I managed to get **Playwright** to work with **Tauri**. The trick is simply to set the `WEBVIEW2_ADDITIONAL_BROWSER_ARGUMENTS` environment variable to `--remote-debugging-port=9222` before launching Tauri. This will allow Playwright to connect to Tauri at `http://localhost:9222`.

Here is my sample C# app doing this:

```csharp
Copy
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using Microsoft.Playwright;
using Microsoft.Playwright.MSTest;

namespace PlaywrightTests;

[TestClass]
public class ExampleTest : PageTest
{
    private static IPage mainPage = null!;
    [ClassInitialize]
    public static async Task LaunchTauri(TestContext context)
    {
        try
        {
            var tauriExePath = Path.Combine("..", "..", "..", "..", "..", "src-tauri", "target", "release", "MyTauriApp.exe");
            var tauriProcess = new System.Diagnostics.Process();
            tauriProcess.StartInfo.FileName = tauriExePath;
            tauriProcess.StartInfo.EnvironmentVariables["WEBVIEW2_ADDITIONAL_BROWSER_ARGUMENTS"] = "--remote-debugging-port=9222";
            tauriProcess.Start();

            // Now start the tests
            var playwright = await Microsoft.Playwright.Playwright.CreateAsync();
            var tauriBrowser = await playwright.Chromium.ConnectOverCDPAsync("http://localhost:9222");
            mainPage = tauriBrowser.Contexts.First().Pages.First();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Assert.Fail($"Failed to connect to WebView2: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task HasTitle()
    {
        await Expect(mainPage).ToHaveTitleAsync(new Regex("My Title"));
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetStartedLink()
    {
        var form = mainPage.Locator("form");
        await form.Locator("#greet-input").FillAsync("Olivier");
        await form.GetByRole(AriaRole.Button, new() { Name = "Greet" }).ClickAsync();
        await Expect(form.Locator("+ p")).ToContainTextAsync("Hello, Olivier! You've been greeted from Rust!");
    }
}
```

# Features & Recipes

Tauri comes with extensibility in mind. On this page you’ll find:

- **[Official Features](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/#official-features)**: Built-in Tauri features and functionality
- **[Community Resources](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/#community-plugins)**: More plugins and recipes built by the Tauri community. You can also contribute your own on [Awesome Tauri](https://github.com/tauri-apps/awesome-tauri)
- **[Support Table](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/#support-table)**: A compatibility table showing which platforms are supported by each official plugin

**Use the search and filter functionality to find features or community resources:**

clear

Platform filter (official features) AllWindowsLinuxmacOSiOSAndroid

#### [Official Features](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/\#official-features)

[Section titled “Official Features”](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/#official-features)

No results found in Official Features

[Autostart](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/autostart/)
Automatically launch your app at system startup.

[Barcode Scanner](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/barcode-scanner/)
Allows your mobile application to use the camera to scan QR codes, EAN-13 and other types of barcodes.

[Biometric](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/biometric/)
Prompt the user for biometric authentication on Android and iOS.

[Clipboard](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/clipboard/)
Read and write to the system clipboard.

[Command Line Interface (CLI)](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/cli/)
Parse arguments from the command line interface.

[Deep Linking](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/deep-linking/)
Set your Tauri application as the default handler for an URL.

[Dialog](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/dialog/)
Native system dialogs for opening and saving files along with message dialogs.

[File System](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/file-system/)
Access the file system.

[Geolocation](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/geolocation/)
Get and track the device's current position, including information about altitude, heading, and speed (if available).

[Global Shortcut](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/global-shortcut/)
Register global shortcuts.

[Haptics](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/haptics/)
Haptic feedback and vibrations on Android and iOS

[HTTP Client](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/http-client/)
Access the HTTP client written in Rust.

[Localhost](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/localhost/)
Use a localhost server in production apps.

[Logging](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/logging/)
Configurable logging.

[NFC](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/nfc/)
Read and write NFC tags on Android and iOS.

[Notifications](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/notification/)
Send native notifications to the user.

[Opener](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/opener/)
Open files and URLs in external applications.

[OS Information](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/os-info/)
Read information about the operating system.

[Persisted Scope](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/persisted-scope/)
Persist runtime scope changes on the filesystem.

[Positioner](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/positioner/)
Move windows to common locations.

[Process](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/process/)
Access the current process.

[Shell](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/shell/)
Access the system shell to spawn child processes.

[Single Instance](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/single-instance/)
Ensure that a single instance of your Tauri app is running at a time.

[SQL](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/sql/)
Tauri Plugin providing an interface for the frontend to communicate with SQL databases through sqlx.

[Store](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/store/)
Persistent key value storage.

[Stronghold](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/stronghold/)
Encrypted, secure database.

[Updater](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/updater/)
In-app updates for Tauri applications.

[Upload](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/upload/)
File uploads through HTTP.

[Websocket](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/websocket/)
Open a WebSocket connection using a Rust client in JavaScript.

[Window State](https://v2.tauri.app/plugin/window-state/)
Persist window sizes and positions.

[tauri-plugin-tracing](https://github.com/fltsci/tauri-plugin-tracing) Structured logging with the tracing crate, featuring JS-to-Rust log bridging, file rotation, and flamegraph profiling.

[tauri-plugin-udp](https://github.com/kuyoonjo/tauri-plugin-udp) UDP socket support.
